summary: Iteration 2: full backend + admin frontend verification. 31/31 pytest cases pass covering auth (login/me/change-password), RBAC (super_admin vs admin vs moderator), users CRUD (self-delete blocked, duplicate email blocked, role/password validation), reviews moderation (public excludes hidden, moderator PATCH allowed but DELETE forbidden), coming-soon CRUD, promo-banner GET/PUT, newsletter, and idempotent super admin seed. Frontend: promo banner renders and closes, coming-soon countdown ticks (D/H/M/S), products bento visible, admin login → overview → Reviews / Coming Soon / Promo / Newsletter / Users pages all render with data, logout clears token, /admin without token redirects to /admin/login.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS is set to allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False. Fine for token-in-header auth, but the auth playbook prefers explicit origins + credentials cookies. Current JWT-in-localStorage design is acceptable.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook recommends lockout after 5 fails). Consider rate limiting.
JWT tokens are stored in localStorage (ur_admin_token) — susceptible to XSS. Playbook prefers httpOnly cookies. Non-blocking for MVP but should be tracked.
server.py is 660 lines; consider splitting into modules (auth, admin, public) as it grows.
admin_update_review/coming-soon/promo-banner accept raw dict payloads instead of Pydantic models — works but weakens validation.
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures create/delete TEST_admin_* and TEST_mod_* users, TEST_ prefixed reviews and coming-soon drops (cleaned up after run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All auth+admin flows verified. Public endpoints: /api/coming-soon, /api/promo-banner, /api/reviews (excludes hidden). Admin base: /api/admin/*. Bearer token in Authorization header. Frontend admin at /admin/*, token key 'ur_admin_token' in localStorage.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
